_comment: Static floor seed for the PUBLIC estate test count (backlog #244). Each entry is a repo with audited tests that does NOT yet self-report a stats.json (Plan 3 rollout). scripts/aggregate-test-scope.sh ADDS these on top of the live-producer rollup — but a live producer with the SAME `repo` slug WINS and the seed entry is dropped (no double-count; the number auto-corrects toward live data as repos start self-reporting). `floor: true` because two counts (obsidian-vault-autopilot, social-scrapers) are themselves floors — the true total is higher, which is why the public tile renders a load-bearing '+'. Phonesis is INTENTIONALLY ABSENT (BLOCKED / diverged clone — internal-only, never in the public count). Source: docs/plans/2026-06-30-homepage-tests-tile-estate-brief.md §3 + neckarshore-planning/docs/reference/test-coverage-matrix.yaml (2026-06-30 UD5 PIR). Counts are floor-framed; the precise per-repo numbers + audited SHAs live in the matrix, not as public hard claims.
_sha_comment: SHA-stamp provenance (Test Charter doctrine — the estate count is auditable, SHA-stamped). `audited_sha` = the Lenin Durchstich SHA whose runner-truth produced THIS row's `total` (or null when no per-repo Durchstich covers the row). `sha_note` = provenance / reason-if-null. Do NOT invent SHAs — an un-audited row stays null. scripts/aggregate-test-scope.sh propagates `audited_sha` (NOT sha_note) into the rollup; scripts/withhold-private-repos.sh then NULLS a PRIVATE repo's audited_sha in the PUBLIC artifact (an internal ref must not leak) — so the seed keeps the full internal audit trail while the served file only exposes PUBLIC repos' SHAs.
